_metadata.description: Syllabus completeness reference — the 9 required syllabus sections (BYU-Idaho template anchor; institution-neutral except Learning Model/Vision) + a first-class generative-AI-policy requirement gate (Stoplight + AI Assessment Scale frameworks) + advisory signals. The announcement layer of the CLO → assessment → rubric chain.
_metadata.runtime_strategy_rationale: Body MD ~4,500 chars / 4 ≈ 1,125 tokens — within the 'embed' band per runtime_strategy_rules. Override to read_at_runtime per named_override_patterns.selective_load: canvas_course_expert loads 2-3 of ~12+ knowledge files per audit task, not all. Selective-load defeats embed-cache benefits even for small files (matches the other knowledge files' strategy).

scope: Whether a course syllabus is COMPLETE — contains the sections students need. A completeness check, not a prose-quality judgment. Tone/clarity/'is each outcome assessed' are human judgments surfaced as advisory data, never pass/fail. Boundaries: outcome well-formedness lives in outcomes_quality_knowledge; the rubric/assessment end lives in rubrics_knowledge; the API surface lives in canvas_api_knowledge / canvas_api_lessons_learned.

facts.definition: (1) Instructor Contact Information (name, office, office hours, email, phone); (2) Overview (course description, credits, outcomes, vision — Vision is BYUI-flavored); (3) Requirements (prerequisites, materials/texts, technology, AI policy); (4) Structure (Learning Model [BYUI-specific], key assessments); (5) Expectations (feedback, workload); (6) Grading (grading scale, late-work policy); (7) Students with Disabilities (accommodation/accessibility statement); (8) University Policies (academic honesty/integrity, Title IX); (9) Disclaimers (subject-to-change / right-to-revise). Institution-neutral except Learning Model and Vision, which are BYUI-specific and flagged as such.
citations: BYU-Idaho Campus Curriculum Development syllabus template (pre_knowledge/byui_learning_teaching/byui_syllabus_guidance.md)

facts.definition: Per BYU-Idaho's AI hub, every course syllabus MUST include a statement about generative AI use. The audit treats AI-policy presence as a first-class required gate that can drive an 'incomplete' verdict on its own — distinct from the section checklist because it is an explicit institutional requirement, not a nice-to-have.
citations: BYU-Idaho AI hub, 'AI in the Syllabus' (byui.edu/ai/academics/ai-in-the-syllabus; pre_knowledge/byui_learning_teaching/byui_ai_hub.md)

facts.term: AI Policy Frameworks (Stoplight / AI Assessment Scale)
facts.definition: Two named frameworks a syllabus may use (detection of which is advisory): Stoplight Framework — RED (disallow) / YELLOW (limited use) / GREEN (active application); AI Assessment Scale — No AI → AI for Ideas → AI for Feedback → AI for Content → AI-Led. The five free-text option statements (No AI / No AI Content Creation / Cite / Open / Case-by-Case) trace to James Helfrich; the Stoplight framework is the BYU-I AI Task Force's. Evidence base: AI detectors are unreliable, so a clear operationalized policy beats policing.

facts.term: Advisory Signals (data only — never drive the verdict)
facts.definition: Syllabus bloat (pitfall #1) — very high word count is a flag, not a failure; vague expectations (pitfall #2) and dry/contractual tone (pitfall #3) — human judgments, out of deterministic scope; outcomes stated — present in syllabus text (deeper mapped+assessed check is outcomes_quality territory); Learning-Model introduced — BYUI-specific.

Section detection is keyword-heuristic on the stripped syllabus HTML. 'Not detected' means REVIEW, not proven-absent — the same evidence-based posture rubric_quality_audit.py takes on Criterion 1. The audit surfaces data for a human decision; it does not certify a syllabus as deficient.

verdict ∈ {complete, incomplete, no_syllabus} + per-section detected[] flags + ai_policy gate (present + detected framework) + advisory signals (bloat / outcomes-stated / learning-model). Emitted by syllabus_audit.py; reads syllabus_body via GET /courses/:id?include[]=syllabus_body.

sources: BYU-Idaho Campus Curriculum Development syllabus template (9 required sections) — pre_knowledge/byui_learning_teaching/byui_syllabus_guidance.md
BYU-Idaho AI hub 'AI in the Syllabus' (byui.edu/ai/academics/ai-in-the-syllabus) — pre_knowledge/byui_learning_teaching/byui_ai_hub.md
General higher-ed syllabus practice (instructor contact, outcomes, materials, grading, accessibility, academic integrity)

pre_run_checklist: Each entry in consumed_by[] resolves to an existing tool/agent
post_run_checklist: knowledge/syllabus_knowledge.json exists with shape=reference and facts[] present
validation.real_course_validation: PENDING. Sandbox-validated only: 16/16 logic checks on synthetic syllabi; live read against CANVAS_SANDBOX_ID (145706) returned 5/9 sections on a real 293-word syllabus_body; exit codes (0/1/2) + --json confirmed 2026-05-22. NOT yet exercised against a real non-sandbox course — hence v0.x; promote to v1.0 after a real syllabus-bearing course run.

changes: Created from the BYUI Learning & Teaching harvest (syllabus template + byui.edu/ai). Grounds syllabus_audit.py. Institution-neutral checklist with BYUI as the anchor profile; BYUI-specific items (Learning Model, Vision) flagged.

Wired into canvas_course_expert.json cross_references with a v0.x-untested caveat. Cataloged in knowledge/README.md flagged v0.1 'help us test' (deliberate exception to the v1.0-only catalog convention). Promotes to v1.0 after a real (non-sandbox) syllabus-bearing course run.

QC-audit fix: brought the JSON onto the house schema used by the other 16 knowledge files — facts converted from an object to a facts[] array (KNW-QC-003), provenance to {sources:[], last_reviewed}, _metadata gained runtime_strategy + runtime_strategy_rationale (KNW-QC-004) + template_version/generated_by/knowledge_id/owner. No substantive content change.
